cohorts.label: 18 prospective acquirers contacted by BofA Merrill Lynch
cohorts.membership_basis: 17 technology companies and one financial sponsor contacted during strategic review

observations.summary: sTec board determined to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec.
An investment bank representing Company A contacted sTec management to request a preliminary meeting to evaluate a possible business transaction.
Company B's President stated intention to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B's management.
Company B's management determined it was not interested in acquiring sTec, believing it could independently develop the needed technology.
Company C expressed a potential interest in exploring an acquisition of particular assets of sTec's business.
Company D contacted sTec management to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.
Board established a special committee to conduct a review of sTec's strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the company.
observations.other_detail: special committee formation

observations.summary: sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
observations.other_detail: financial advisor engagement letter

observations.summary: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E.
observations.other_detail: included dont ask dont waive provision

observations.summary: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F.
observations.other_detail: included dont ask dont waive provision

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting 18 prospective acquirers of sTec at the direction of the special committee.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G.
sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
observations.other_detail: addendum to existing NDA

observations.summary: Company C indicated it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of the company; the special committee decided not to continue discussions.
observations.other_detail: interested only in select assets

observations.summary: Company F declined to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec.
observations.other_detail: interested only in select assets

date.resolution_note: Shortly after April 24 Company F declined

observations.summary: Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec; the special committee decided not to continue discussions.
observations.other_detail: interested only in select assets

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.
Company D provided a verbal indication of interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
observations.other_detail: verbal indication; price greater than $5.60 per share

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.
Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 - $7.10 per share in cash.
Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
observations.other_detail: included dont ask dont waive provision

observations.summary: Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.
observations.other_detail: included draft exclusivity agreement

observations.summary: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 - $5.75 per share in cash.

date.resolution_note: Between Company H's May 15 IOI and the May 16 board meeting

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch contacted Company H and indicated that the price range submitted was not sufficient to move them forward in the process; Company H could submit a revised indication of interest.
observations.other_detail: price insufficient to advance; invited to revise

date.resolution_note: After the board meeting on May 16

observations.summary: BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
observations.other_detail: final round process letters

observations.summary: Company H indicated it remained interested in a potential acquisition but was not able to increase its indicated value range.
WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash, with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements.
observations.other_detail: included mark-up of merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements

observations.summary: Company D indicated continued interest in a potential acquisition and understood they needed to move meaningfully on value, but needed approximately two weeks of additional time for due diligence and to submit a written indication of interest.
observations.other_detail: requested two additional weeks

observations.summary: Board directed BofA Merrill Lynch to request a best and final proposal from WDC and a best and final written proposal from Company D by May 30, 2013.
WDC verbally indicated that its indication of interest at $9.15 per share was WDC's best possible price, reiterating concern regarding the price.
observations.other_detail: verbal reconfirmation of $9.15 as best possible price

observations.summary: Company D reconfirmed they would require additional time to submit a written indication of interest but indicated they could be competitive on price if given additional time.
observations.other_detail: requested additional time; believed they could be competitive on price

observations.summary: WDC communicated it was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec and was not prepared to move forward with a transaction at that time; WDC discontinued due diligence efforts. Board held a special meeting to discuss potential next steps.
observations.other_detail: reevaluating interest; discontinued due diligence

observations.summary: Company D indicated it would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.
observations.other_detail: disengaging from process

observations.summary: WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest at $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash, lowering from its prior $9.15 indication, citing business factors and a change in bidding strategy.
observations.other_detail: reduced from $9.15; WDC stated acquisition not essential to its SSD strategy

observations.summary: Board directed BofA Merrill Lynch to communicate that the board would not respond to a proposal with a range and that WDC must submit a proposal reflecting its best offer with confirmation of senior management support.
observations.other_detail: required specific best offer with senior management confirmation

observations.summary: WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash, described as WDC's best and final price.
observations.other_detail: described as best and final price

observations.summary: sTec and WDC entered into a clean team agreement to allow certain representatives of WDC access to further information in the data room.
sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement.
sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement at $6.85 per share in cash.
WDC and sTec announced the execution of the merger agreement in a joint press release prior to the opening of trading.
observations.other_detail: public announcement of merger agreement execution

observations.summary: Balch Hill Partners filed a Schedule 13D with the SEC reporting a 6.4% stake in sTec and subsequently launched an activist campaign urging the board to reduce costs, focus products, and explore strategic alternatives, including nominating directors for election.
observations.other_detail: Schedule 13D filing and activist campaign urging strategic alternatives

observations.summary: sTec board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch as financial advisor and determined to confidentially approach strategic buyers most likely to have interest in a potential acquisition, while also conducting a limited confirmatory exploration of financial buyer interest.

exclusions.explanation: Pre-2012 strategic considerations, informal third-party approaches, and 2011 lunch meetings between Moshayedi and WDC executives predate the formal sale process and involve no specific transaction terms.

Windows w2 and w3 cover board rationale, fairness opinion valuation analysis, comparable transactions, and BofA Merrill Lynch advisory fee disclosures. These contain no extractable chronology observations.
